articles.title: Trump fires the entire National Science Board
articles.summary: The Trump administration has dismissed the entire National Science Board, which advises on the National Science Foundation. The NSF has been fundamental in developing technology used in MRIs, cellphones, and helping startups like Duolingo.

articles.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant's hardware strategy
articles.summary: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware-focused leader, signaling that Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy. This represents a potential shift in Apple's strategic direction under new leadership.

articles.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
articles.summary: Canadian AI startup Cohere is acquiring Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Schwarz Group. The companies aim to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

articles.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
articles.summary: Anthropic conducted an experiment where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers in a classified marketplace. The agents struck real deals for real goods using real money, demonstrating advanced AI commerce capabilities.

articles.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
articles.summary: Sam Altman issued a letter apologizing to residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, saying he is "deeply sorry" that OpenAI failed to alert law enforcement about a suspect in a recent mass shooting. This suggests OpenAI had relevant information about the incident.

articles.title: An influx of used EVs could drive down prices
articles.summary: Used EV availability is expected to surge from 123,000 expired leases in 2025 to over 600,000 by 2027-2028. This influx could dramatically reduce EV prices and make electric vehicles more accessible to consumers.

articles.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
articles.summary: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 features four defined technology domains with live demonstrations and dedicated exhibit floors. The event showcases the people actually building and funding technologies globally.

articles.title: Maine's governor vetoes data center moratorium
articles.summary: L.D. 307, which would have imposed the country's first statewide moratorium on new data centers until November 2027, was vetoed by Maine's governor. This keeps the door open for data center development in the state.

articles.title: Researchers say we're talking less than ever
articles.summary: University researchers found that spoken words between humans fell by nearly 28% between 2005 and 2019, from an average of 16,632 words daily. The decline has likely worsened since the pandemic, based on data from over 2,000 people.

articles.title: After three months on Linux, I don't miss Windows at all
articles.summary: A user switched to Linux as their main desktop OS and has only booted Windows twice in three months - once for scanning and once for photo printing. The experiment demonstrates Linux's viability as a primary operating system for everyday users.

articles.title: Sharge's fast Qi2.2 MagSafe battery is down to $70 with a free USB-C cable
articles.summary: The Sharge Icemag 3 is a 10,000mAh magnetic Qi2 power bank that wirelessly outputs 25W to iPhone 16-series phones. It includes a built-in kickstand and is currently on sale for $70 with a free USB-C cable.
